Powered byOutside

IZU, JAPAN - AUGUST 03: Chloe Dygert, Megan Jastrab, Jennifer Valente, Emma White of Team United States sprint during the Women's team pursuit finals, bronze medal of the Track Cycling on day eleven of the Tokyo 2020 Olympic Games at Izu Velodrome on August 03, 2021 in Izu, Japan. (Photo by Justin Setterfield/Getty Images)

The 2024 Summer Olympics kick off Friday, July 26 in Paris, France. There are five cycling disciplines at this year’s Olympics — road, mountain, track, BMX freestyle, and BMX racing — and a cycling event to watch every single day of the Games. There will be 22 events across the five disciplines.



Both the men’s and women’s time trials kick off the Olympic cycling events on Saturday, July 27, followed by cross-country mountain biking on Sunday and Monday. The men’s and women’s road races follow one week later.

Read also:These are the cyclists Team USA is sending to the Olympics

The cycling events of the 2024 Summer Olympics in Paris are scheduled to run at four different venues: Pont d’Iéna for road and time trial races; Vélodrome de Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines for track cycling and BMX racing; Élancourt Hill for mountain biking; and Place de la Concorde for the BMX freestyle.

In the United States, the Olympics will beshown on TV on NBCandstreamed on Peacock. We’ve put together a calendar of when each event will shown; the full streaming schedule of all cycling events can be foundhere.

title: How to Watch Cycling During the 2024 Summer Olympics in Paris, France
author: Betsy Welch
description: There are olympic cycling events every day of the Paris games
